text: Hello everyone, my name is Dr. Sanket Pissatt and I am a consultant gynecological minimal access surgeon practicing in Mumbai, India. The initial part of the video is going to describe the anatomy of the female reproductive system which has the uterus in the center and a pair of tubes and ovaries on either side. In the front the uterus is related to the urinary bladder which creates the urine and behind is related to the rectum. So there are several important vital structures which are lying in close relation to the uterus which have to be remembered during laparoscopic hysterectomy which is also called uterus removal surgery.

The surgeon begins the laparoscopic hysterectomy by dividing the corneal support structures of the uterus using electrocautery. The peritoneal covering, known as the broad ligament, is then divided. The urinary bladder is carefully separated from the uterus to prevent damage. The uterine artery on the left side is divided, followed by the same procedure on the right side.

After dividing the uterine artery, the surgeon incises the vagina, separating the cervix from the vaginal tissue. The fallopian tubes are removed, while the ovaries may be preserved depending on the patient's age. The vagina is then sutured using absorbable sutures for reconstruction.
